Resulting From: Overlaps among housing, energy, and social policies without a unified renovation roadmap.

Barrier: Lack of interest on the part of building decision-makers in financial instruments
Resulting From: Grant culture with 100% grants in prior renovation programs discourages use of financial instruments
No detailed segmentation by type, age, and performance of single-family homes.
Energy performance standards are poorly enforced for existing homes.

Barrier: No regulations regarding banning the use of wood or coal as heating fuel
Resulting From: Many SFH use wood or coal fuel for heating. It is desirable to substitute these polluting fuels with environment-friendly alternatives but there are no financial incentives for owners of SFH to make such substitution.

Barrier: Lack of delivery mechanisms for scaling up
Resulting From: There are no formal programs addressing large-scale renovation of single family residential buildings
Many municipalities lack staff and technical expertise to assist homeowners.
Housing, energy, and finance ministries operate separately with limited coordination

Barrier: Limited willingness and capacities of homeowners to engage in renovation projects
Resulting From: Limited borrowing capacity, lack of awareness on the benefits of EE and limited number of success stories

Barrier: Limited technical advice and project support
Resulting From: Few advisory services for households on design and financing.

Barrier: High upfront investment cost for building renovation
Resulting From: Renovation costs are expensive relative to average household incomes
Few banks offer suitable credit lines with affordable financing terms for homeowners or low-income groups.

Banks require the borrowers to develop a baseline and prove that minimum efficiency requirements have been met. The costs of these activities result in high transaction costs, as well time and effort commitment, and deters SFH owners from seeking bank loans.
Owners and tenants have different incentives
Vulnerable and low-income groups cannot afford to repay renovation loans.
Skilled labor scarce in rural areas; most firms focus on shallow renovations.
Some renovation projects are poorly executed
